llm_result.summary: The case presents a recurring cycle where headaches trigger acute negative mood, leading to social withdrawal, guilt, and further stress that may intensify headache burden. Targeted, modifiable lever-variables across biological, psychological, and social domains (and their interactions) can disrupt the pain–mood–social cycle by reducing headache frequency/intensity, dampening maladaptive emotional reactivity, re-engaging valued social activities, and leveraging self-monitoring to tailor interventions. The proposed solutions emphasize behavioral activation, relaxation and sleep optimization, mindfulness/acceptance, self-compassion, social connectedness, nutrition/hydration, gentle movement, and measurement-based feedback to create a multi-domain improvement trajectory while avoiding unsafe practices or medications. For safety, ongoing risk assessment and escalation plans are included if clinical risk signs appear.

solutions.predictor_variable: Structured Behavioral Activation with Social Re-engagement and Activity Pacing
solutions.solution_candidate: Weekly graded schedule of 2–3 brief social/meaningful activities with paced activation, aiming to re-establish engagement and reduce avoidance.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Increases positive reinforcement, reduces social withdrawal and avoidance, and breaks the pain–shame cycle by promoting controllable activities and mood improvement.
solutions.measurement: Adherence to planned activities (days/week with activity), number of social engagements per week, mood rating during/after headache episodes (0–10)
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for fatigue or overexertion; tailor activities to headache status; adjust plan if headaches worsen.
solutions.mapping_query: Targets C04 (avoidance) and C07 (pain–shame cycle) and C03 (mood drop) via increased engagement and activation.

solutions.predictor_variable: Breathwork + Progressive Muscle Relaxation (PMR) for Pain Modulation
solutions.solution_candidate: Daily guided diaphragmatic breathing with PMR (15–20 minutes) during headaches or as a preemptive routine.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Parasympathetic activation and muscular relaxation reduce peak pain intensity and distress, improving mood during episodes.
solutions.measurement: Headache intensity (0–10) during episodes; mood rating during episodes (0–10); headache frequency
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure comfortable practice; do not replace medical advice if headaches worsen.
solutions.mapping_query: Aims to lower C02 (intensity) and mitigate C03 (acute mood collapse) through physiological relaxation.

solutions.predictor_variable: Mindfulness-Based Pain Acceptance / ACT for Pain Reactivity
solutions.solution_candidate: 8-week mindfulness/ACT-driven program targeting pain reactivity, acceptance, values-aligned actions, and reduced avoidance.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Decouples pain from affective distress, reduces avoidance, and lowers reactivity to pain, thereby weakening the pain–shame loop.
solutions.measurement: Pain distress (0–10), mood during episodes (0–10), number of avoidance moments per week
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for emotional distress surges; provide facilitator support if needed.
solutions.mapping_query: Addresses C03 (acute mood) and C04/C07 (avoidance/shame) via acceptance and value-based action.

solutions.solution_candidate: Fixed wake time, consistent bed routine, morning light exposure, and sleep environment optimization to stabilize sleep/wake patterns.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep reduces headache vulnerability and stabilizes mood/reactivity; better energy supports social engagement.
solutions.measurement: Sleep duration/regularity (hours), number of headache days, average headache intensity, daily mood rating
solutions.safety_notes: Consider shift work or comorbid sleep disorders; adjust plan accordingly.
solutions.mapping_query: Expected to reduce headache frequency (C01), lower intensity (C02), and support mood stability (C03) via circadian regulation.

solutions.solution_candidate: 2-part program: self-compassion exercises and cognitive reframing to soften guilt after cancellations and reduce self-criticism.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces guilt/self-blame (C05), mitigates social withdrawal (C06), and dampens the pain–shame feedback loop (C07) by reframing experiences and building kindness.
solutions.measurement: Guilt intensity after cancellations, perceived isolation, mood during episodes
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for rumination; adjust if distress increases.
solutions.mapping_query: Targets C05 (guilt) and C06 (isolation) and C07 (pain–shame cycle) through self-compassion and cognitive reframing.

solutions.predictor_variable: Structured Social Support & Community Engagement (Social Prescribing)
solutions.solution_candidate: Connect to community groups/activities aligned with interests; leverage social-prescribing pathways to rebuild meaningful social ties.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Re-establishes social belonging, reduces isolation, and provides positive reinforcement that can offset pain-related mood drops.
solutions.measurement: Number of social engagements per week; self-reported isolation; mood during/after social interactions
solutions.safety_notes: Assess accessibility and safety; avoid overcommitment; ensure consent.
solutions.mapping_query: Addresses C04 and C06 by rebuilding social connectedness and reducing withdrawal.

solutions.solution_candidate: Dietary pattern (Mediterranean/DASH) with hydration goals; reduce pro-inflammatory foods; monitor micronutrient status.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Nutritional pattern influences systemic inflammation and energy, potentially lowering headache burden and improving mood and social energy.
solutions.measurement: Headache days, average intensity, self-reported mood, hydration adequacy
solutions.safety_notes: Coordinate with nutritional needs/allergies; avoid drastic restrictions; do not replace medical evaluation.
solutions.mapping_query: Diet and hydration quality may reduce headache frequency/intensity and improve mood/social energy (C01/C02/C06).

solutions.predictor_variable: Gentle Movement, Posture Ergonomics & Somatic Care
solutions.solution_candidate: Daily 10–15 minute neck/shoulder stretches, posture checks, and light walking; ergonomic adjustments.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces muscular tension contributing to headaches; improves daytime energy and mood, supporting social engagement.
solutions.measurement: Headache frequency and intensity; fatigue; activity tolerance
solutions.safety_notes: Discontinue if pain worsens; tailor to condition; gradual progression.
solutions.mapping_query: Targets C01/C02 by reducing tension and improving physical readiness for social/psychological interventions.

solutions.solution_candidate: Daily or thrice-weekly symptom/activity logging with automatic feedback, goal setting, and adaptive plan adjustments.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Continuous data informs personalized adjustments, enhances engagement, and accelerates cycle-breaking by aligning predictors with real-time patterns.
solutions.measurement: Daily headache days, intensity, mood, social activity, plan adherence; trend analyses
solutions.safety_notes: Protect data privacy; ensure supportive interpretation; escalate if risk signals appear.
solutions.mapping_query: Utilizes ongoing monitoring to tailor multi-domain interventions and track effects on headaches and mood cycle.

solutions.solution_candidate: Moderate caffeine intake with warnings; reduce or optimize alcohol use; ensure hydration and balanced pattern.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Stimulant and depressant fluctuations can influence headache frequency/severity and mood; stabilization supports social functioning.
solutions.measurement: Caffeine/alcohol consumption; headache days; mood; social activity
solutions.safety_notes: If symptoms worsen, adjust; discuss with clinician; avoid abrupt withdrawal effects.
solutions.mapping_query: Altering caffeine/alcohol patterns aims to reduce headache burden, mood fluctuation, and social withdrawal.
